retry_reason: Failure of refcount_inc_not_zero means the neighbour's refcount is zero (object dying/dead); retrying will not change the outcome because the object is gone.
contract_summary: RCU-protected get-wrapper: looks up a neighbour entry by device and key, and if found, attempts to acquire a reference via refcount_inc_not_zero. Returns non-NULL only if the object is live and the reference was successfully incremented. The caller must check the return value; if non-NULL, the caller owns a reference and must release it with a corresponding neighbour_put (e.g., neigh_release). If NULL is returned, the neighbour entry does not exist or is being destroyed — no reference is acquired and the caller should not use a stale pointer. The RCU read lock is held only during the lookup and refcount attempt.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to look up a neighbour entry and conditionally acquire a reference via refcount_inc_not_zero; the only non-refcount logic is the RCU lock/unlock which is merely infrastructure for the refcount operation.
judge.retry_reason: Failure of refcount_inc_not_zero means the neighbour's refcount is zero (object dying/dead); retrying will not change the outcome because the object is gone.

check.reason: refcount_inc_not_zero operates on n->refcnt, which is the refcount field of the returned neighbour pointer n. Access path n.refcnt.refs correctly traces from the return variable to the atomic_t member. The type chain neighbour--refcount_t--atomic_t matches the struct definitions, and the segment counts (3) are equal. The operation is a get on the return value.
